lessons.title: Current Actions
lessons.topic: Describing a busy scene in a playground
lessons.context: Describing a busy scene in a playground.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Where do children play outside at school?
questions.hint: It's a fun place outside with swings and slides!

questions.question_text: What can you do on a playground?
questions.hint: Think about the fun things you can do outside with your friends!

questions.question_text: When is a playground busy?
questions.hint: Busy means lots of people doing things at the same time!

items.definition: Having fun with toys or friends.
having fun with toys or games

steps.title: A Busy Playground
steps.text: It is a sunny day at the playground. Sam is playing on the swings. Emma is eating an apple under a tree. Tom is drinking juice on the bench. Lily is wearing her new red hat. Everyone is having fun!
steps.scene_description: children playing at outdoor playground on sunny day, kids on swings and benches

questions.question: Where is Emma eating her apple?

steps.rule_pattern: I am/He is/She is/They are ___ + ing
steps.explanation: We use am, is, or are with an -ing verb to talk about what is happening right now!
examples: I am playing on the slide.

items.sentence: I am playing on the slide.
items.corrected: I am playing on the slide.

items.question: I ___ eating breakfast right now.
items.explanation: Use 'am' with I in present continuous

items.question: She ___ drinking orange juice.
items.explanation: Use 'is' with she in present continuous

items.question: The children ___ playing outside.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with plural nouns in present continuous

items.question: My friend ___ wearing red shoes.
items.explanation: Use 'is' with he/she in present continuous

items.question: We ___ eating ice cream now.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with we in present continuous

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Look at the playground today. Can you see the boy? He is wearing a yellow t-shirt and he is playing on the swings. The girl with the pink hat is eating a sandwich. Two friends are sitting on the bench and they are drinking water together. Everyone is having a great time!

questions.question: What color is the boy's t-shirt?
What is the girl with the pink hat doing?
How many friends are sitting on the bench?

steps.prompt: Look at the playground picture and tell me what the children are doing!

prompts.prompt: What is the boy doing on the swing?
prompts.expected_text: He is playing on the swing.

prompts.prompt: Tell me about the children on the bench. What are they doing and what are they wearing?
prompts.expected_text: They are drinking water and wearing hats.

questions.question_text: Which word means 'putting food in your mouth'?
She ___ drinking juice right now.
Read: 'The boy is playing on the slide.' What is the boy doing?
True or False: 'They am playing' is correct.
I ___ wearing my red shoes today.

lessons.title: At Home Actions
lessons.topic: A phone call asking family members what they are doing
lessons.context: A phone call asking family members what they are doing.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you use to call your family?
questions.hint: You hold it in your hand and talk into it!

questions.question_text: Who can you call at home?
questions.hint: Think about the people in your family!

questions.question_text: What can you ask your family on the phone?
questions.hint: You want to know about their action right now!

items.definition: The time when you are not awake.
Making pictures with pencils or crayons.

items.hint: You do this in bed at night
You make pictures with a pencil

items.sentence: Shh! The baby is ___ in the bedroom.
Dad is ___ in the living room.
My sister is ___ with her headphones.
My brother is ___ a cartoon show.

steps.title: Family Phone Call
steps.text: Lucy calls home. Mom is drawing a picture. Dad is watching TV in the living room. Her brother Tom is listening to music. Grandma is sleeping in her bedroom. Everyone is busy!
steps.scene_description: young girl talking on phone at home with family members doing different activities

steps.rule_pattern: I am ___(verb)ing. / He/She is ___(verb)ing. / We/They are ___(verb)ing.
steps.explanation: We use am, is, or are with verb+ing to talk about what someone is doing right now.

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Let me tell you about my family right now. My mom is drawing a beautiful picture. My dad is watching TV in the living room. My little sister is sleeping in her bedroom. And my big brother? He is listening to music with his headphones!

steps.prompt: Practice talking about what people are doing right now. Listen and repeat, then say your own sentence!

prompts.prompt: Now tell me: What is Dad doing? (He is...)

questions.question_text: What does this emoji mean? 😴
Mom ___ drawing a picture now.
Read: Dad is watching TV. Question: What is Dad doing?
True or False: We say 'I am listening' not 'I is listening'.
Which sentence is correct?

lessons.title: Grammar Focus
lessons.context: Focus on -ing endings and subject-verb agreement.

steps.instruction: Let's think about actions! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: When you mime, what do you do?
questions.hint: Think about showing something with your body, not your voice!

questions.question_text: Which action can you mime with your arms?
questions.hint: What can you do by moving your arms up and down?

questions.question_text: In a mime game, how do friends guess your action?
questions.hint: Your friends need to see what you're doing!

items.definition: Looking at words and understanding them.

steps.title: Our Fun Mime Game
steps.text: Today we are playing a mime game! Emma is running fast. Tom is singing a song. Lily is dancing with her arms up. Ben is reading a book quietly. We are guessing the actions!
steps.scene_description: diverse children playing mime game in classroom, acting out different actions, happy and engaged

questions.question: How many children are playing the mime game?

steps.rule_pattern: I am ___ing. You are ___ing. He/She is ___ing.
steps.explanation: We use am, is, or are with -ing words to show what someone is doing RIGHT NOW!
examples: She is singing a happy song.
They are dancing in the park.

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Let's play our mime game now. I am thinking of an action. Watch me carefully. I am moving my arms up and down. I am going very fast. Can you guess? Yes! I am running!

steps.prompt: Let's practice speaking! Say these sentences about what you are doing.

prompts.prompt: Tell me what you are doing right now using 'I am...'
prompts.expected_text: I am sitting/I am listening/I am learning

questions.question_text: Which action uses your voice?
I ___ running to school now.
She ___ dancing in the park.
True or False: In a mime game, you talk a lot.
What are the children doing? 'The children ___ reading books.'

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Describing a picture of people doing various activities
lessons.context: Describing a picture of people doing various activities.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What are you doing right now?
questions.hint: Look around you! You're sitting and studying with me!

questions.question_text: If your friend is throwing a ball, what is your friend doing?
questions.hint: When you throw a ball, you are having fun! What do we call that?

questions.question_text: Look at this picture: 🏃. What is this person doing?
questions.hint: Look at the legs! They are moving very fast!

items.definition: Putting food in your mouth and chewing it
Putting a liquid in your mouth and swallowing it
Having fun with toys or games
Closing your eyes and resting in bed
Moving very fast with your legs
Looking at words in a book
Making pictures with pencils or crayons
Looking at shows on a screen

steps.title: A Busy Day at Home
steps.text: It is Saturday morning. Mum is drinking coffee in the kitchen. Dad is reading a book on the sofa. Tom is playing with his toy cars. His sister Lucy is drawing a picture. The baby is sleeping in the bedroom. Everyone is doing something different!
steps.scene_description: happy family doing different activities at home, parents and children in living room

questions.question: Is Dad playing with toy cars?

steps.explanation: We use am, is, or are plus a verb with -ing to tell what someone is doing RIGHT NOW!
examples: I am eating an apple right now.
She is playing with her doll.
They are running in the park.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with she, he, or it

items.sentence: I is playing with my toys.

items.explanation: Use 'is' with he, she, or it

items.sentence: They is running in the park.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with they, we, or you
Use 'is' with one animal or person
Use 'are' with we, they, or you

items.question: She ___ wearing a hat.
items.explanation: Use 'is' with she, he, or it

items.question: They ___ playing in the garden.
items.explanation: Use 'are' with they, we, or you

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Look at this picture. Can you see the family? The dad is reading a newspaper. The mum is drinking tea. The boy is playing with his toy car. The girl is drawing a picture of a house. What a busy family!

questions.question: What is the dad doing?
options: He is playing with a toy car

steps.prompt: Look at the picture and tell me what the people are doing!

prompts.prompt: What are you doing right now?
Look at this picture. What is the boy doing?
Tell me about this family. What are they doing?
prompts.expected_text: The mum is cooking and the dad is reading

questions.question_text: What is the boy doing? 🏃
I ___ eating an apple right now.
She ___ listening to music.
True or False: 'They are playing' is correct grammar.
Read: 'The girl is drawing a picture.' What is the girl doing?
